title: Commemorating the heroic sacrifices of the people of Ukraine 3 years after Russian President Vladimir Putin's illegal and unprovoked war against Ukraine on February 24, 2022, and recognizing the terrible cost of Russia's committing crimes against Humanity aggression.
plain_summary: This bill honors the people of Ukraine three years after Russia started an illegal war against them on February 24, 2022. The bill condemns Russian President Vladimir Putin and Russia for committing war crimes, including killing over 12,000 innocent civilians and kidnapping 19,000 children. The House of Representatives promises to keep supporting Ukraine so they can defend themselves and get back all their land that Russia took.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Foreign Affairs.

sections.title: Resolution Title
sections.text: This resolution honors the brave sacrifices of the people of Ukraine three years after Russian President Vladimir Putin started his illegal and unprovoked war against Ukraine on February twenty fourth, two thousand twenty two. It also recognizes the terrible cost of Russia's crimes against humanity and aggression.

sections.title: Background Facts
sections.text: Russia first invaded the independent nation of Ukraine in two thousand fourteen without any reason when it illegally took control of Crimea, Donetsk, and Luhansk regions. On February twenty fourth, two thousand twenty two, Russian President Vladimir Putin and his government launched a violent full scale invasion of Ukraine. Russia's full scale invasion of Ukraine was meant to help Vladimir Putin achieve his goal of taking over all of independent Ukraine, but this goal has failed and will never succeed. After three years of war in Ukraine, the people of Ukraine continue to show their commitment to defeating Russia and building a fair and free democratic country. The war being carried out against Ukraine today happens in the context of a long history of occupation and persecution by authoritarian powers including Russia, the Soviet Union, and Nazi Germany. Over twelve thousand Ukrainian civilians have been killed and nearly twenty nine thousand one hundred seventy eight injured by Russia's unjustified war of aggression, wounded by deliberate and random Russian attacks against Ukraine. Over nineteen thousand Ukrainian born children have been illegally kidnapped by Russia and sympathizers and taken to Russian occupied territory in Ukraine or Russia in an attempt to forcibly turn them into Russian citizens and erase their Ukrainian heritage and identities. The full scale war in Ukraine has forced nearly four million people to move inside the country and six point eight million people into neighboring countries or other countries around the world. More than fourteen million six hundred thousand people need humanitarian assistance in Ukraine, representing over one quarter of Ukraine's pre war population. The total value of direct damage and destruction to Ukraine's infrastructure as a result of Russia's full scale war to educational institutions, museums, religious institutions, energy sector, and cultural sites, medical facilities, residential buildings and homes, entertainment arenas, digital and telecommunications sector, agricultural sector, businesses, government buildings and more is estimated to be over four hundred billion dollars. Over one hundred thousand instances of crimes against humanity and war crimes committed by Russian forces, including the intentional targeting of civilians, sexual violence, torture, mass murder, and other crimes against humanity, have been recorded and verified. The international community expressed concerns about the whereabouts and treatment of Ukrainians in Russian filtration camps (places where people are detained and sorted) who were possibly detained in unknown locations at high risk of torture and ill treatment. The International Criminal Court (a court that prosecutes war crimes) has charged Vladimir Putin for his role in the war crime of unlawful deportation of children and transfer from Ukraine to Russia, and the United States has determined that Russia committed crimes against humanity during its war in Ukraine. The involvement of China, Iran, and North Korea in furthering the violence being committed against the people of Ukraine is evidence of the increasing coordination of an emerging Axis of Tyranny, whose primary intention is to sow instability at the expense of innocent civilians and as a means of undermining the security of the free world. Aside from individual international volunteers, Ukraine has fought this war entirely with Ukrainian personnel. With aid from the United States and its allies, Ukraine has liberated territories that were once occupied by Russia. Ukraine has destroyed as much as forty percent of Russia's entire fighting military forces, including over three hundred thousand Russian casualties or as much as ninety percent of Russia's estimated initial invasion force. Since Ukraine's successful repelling of Russia's initial invasion, the Ukrainian people have inspired the world through its heroic stand for democratic values.

sections.title: House of Representatives Resolutions
sections.text: The House of Representatives reaffirms its longtime commitment to the people of Ukraine and strong support for Ukraine as it defends itself against Russian aggression. The House unequivocally condemns Vladimir Putin and his authoritarian regime. The House stands in solidarity with the people of Ukraine in commemoration of the terrible costs Ukraine has borne after three years of brutal war. The House recognizes the importance of Ukraine's historic fight for the security and prosperity of all nations on the continent of Europe and the United States. The House commends the people of Ukraine who are sacrificing greatly as they seek to rejoin the world community of free, independent, and democratic nations. The House stands with the people of Ukraine to continue a range of substantive measures to prevent external direct and hybrid aggression against Ukraine and hold Russia accountable for aggression and violations of international law. The House reaffirms to never recognize Russia's attempted annexation (taking control) of Crimea and full support for international efforts, including in the Normandy Format (a diplomatic process), aimed at negotiating a diplomatic resolution to the Russia led armed conflict in the Donetsk and Luhansk regions of Ukraine on the basis of respect for international law, including the UN Charter. The House expresses the need to restore Ukrainian control over the Ukrainian territories temporarily occupied or controlled by Russia including Crimea and the Donetsk and Luhansk regions. The House confirms the importance of the Budapest Memorandum of December fifth, nineteen ninety four (an agreement where Russia promised to respect Ukraine's borders), and calls on Russia to fulfill its previous commitments to respect Ukraine's sovereignty and territorial integrity within its internationally recognized borders. The House works on building Ukraine's resilience against Russian aggression and reaffirmed the importance of the Minsk agreements (peace agreements) to end Russian aggression against Ukraine. The House supports creating security conditions for the full implementation of the Minsk Agreements. The House aims at deepening Ukraine's integration into Euro Atlantic institutions concurrent priorities. The House supports the United States Ukraine Strategic Partnership Charter as a platform to advance United States and Ukraine strategic interests.
